title: gonystylus macrocarpus
intro: Gonystylus macrocarpus, also known as the Macrocarpous Gonystylus or Ramin, is a plant species in the family Thymelaeaceae, native to Southeast Asia and Australia. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Gonystylus macrocarpus include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, Gonystylus macrocarpus is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
